# Transcrição: 10. Relationship and the Close

## [00:00 - 03:04]

You know, a person at our school once told me, commented to me, he says, Grant, you put so much value on the clothes. It seems like you spend so much energy on the clothes and not enough on the relationship. And I said, oh, wow, I'm sorry, you know, that you thought that. Because the reality is, without the trust, the good feelings and the relationship, the clothes is impossible. So you must have missed our earlier school because in the basic setup of fundamentals of selling, man, the relationship is everything. However, the relationship by itself will not get you a close. Look, most deals only close when the salesperson closes the deal, not because someone likes you. Nobody says to you, oh, I like you, let's do it. Most people need some help in getting it done. They don't buy from you, your company, they don't buy your ideas or even your presentation. When somebody called you up lately and said, hey, I want an appointment with you, I want some time to see you so you can pitch me, that doesn't happen, folks. I don't care how strong your relationship is, somebody has to close that deal. That is why we made the first rule of selling about agreement. Remember that? I agree. I'm with you. I understand. Try that. I agree. I'm with you. I understand. Why? Because it develops a relationship. Remember, the difference between a contact person and a contract is what? The R relationship. And the more they like you, the more you agree with them and the more they agree with you, the easier it is to close that deal. Likes do not, how they say likes a track and unlike things, push apart and unlike things in science attract, no, that's not true. In selling, likes a track, agreement attracts, you want agreement. But just because they like you, just because they agree with you doesn't mean they'll close until somebody does that. People do not separate from their money. That thing that they find so valuable in life, okay? They do not make decisions requiring future obligations without some help from the closer. People are highly insecure in our society today, highly insecure about their money and their ability to make good decisions. Remember this. People are highly insecure in our environment today about two things. Their money, the ability to create money, produce money, and number two, make decisions. And that's where your job becomes of such a service and so valuable to people to help them overcome their fear of money and their inability or lack of confidence in their ability to make decisions, good decisions. People are more likely to flow to people they like, I think we agree with this, flow money to people they like, flow energy, effort, support to people they like than people they don't like. So a relationship is critical. That's why agreement is critical, okay? People are more likely to flow to people they agree with than people they disagree with. But don't confuse that with, oh, I'm just going to agree with people, create relationships, I'm going to be nice. Look if the trick was just being nice, there'd be about four billion people on this planet

that are rich. That's not the case. Most people are nice. Only maybe 20% of the population is nasty and not nice. I know I'm actually helping this guy finish. See, most salespeople spend too much time on nice and never actually transitioning over into the close. Did you see the transition? You're my buddy. You're my pal. We're my friend. Boom, let's roll. I got to transition and get that actionable exchange of value. Where do I get exchange? Where do I get real value? When I close, not when I'm nice to people. Being nice to people is expected in our culture. Being nice to people typically doesn't get anything done. I'm suggesting you learn how to close. How you get people to make a decision now and quit thinking that nice is going to get you to close. ## [06:04 - 06:40]

Number two, persist. And number three, persist some more. Four, you will have to handle objections, stalls, and complaints. I don't care if this guy is your husband or your wife or your best friend or your brother or sister. I've closed all those scenarios.
